rationale.R1.2: Metric definition is circular ('cost-adjusted accuracy…captures the trade-off…because it captures the trade-off'); the paragraph restates its own logic rather than advancing it, indicating consistency problems.
rationale.R1.3: Preliminary results claiming 18–24% improvement are asserted with no pointer to a table, figure, or appendix, making evidence-claim alignment a live concern.
rationale.R1.5: Severe structural redundancy: ADMET categories listed twice verbatim, 'three task families' referenced four times, 'cost-adjusted accuracy' definition and motivation restated three times — signals a reorganization responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: Nearly every sentence contains a near-verbatim restatement of the preceding sentence; this is the most obvious and pervasive defect in the passage, making writing polish load-bearing for any reasonable interpretation of 'take a look.'

parsed.clarification_question: Is your primary goal here a writing/polish review (fixing redundancy and prose quality), or a technical/logical review (assessing whether the benchmark design, metric definition, and results claims are sound)?
